title: Improved Extraction Methods to Isolate High Molecular Weight DNA From Magnaporthaceae and Other Grass Root Fungi for Long-Read Whole Genome Sequencing
author: Michelle J. Grey
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML
materials: Cytiva Nucleon PhytoPure, pack of 50 × 1.0 g
Macherey-Nagel NucleoBond HMW DNA
70% ethanol solution (EtOH) (see Recipes) Recipes

Liquid nitrogen (CAS

Preheat the buffer H1 to 55 °C.
Discard the flowthrough.
F. Precipitate DNA
Carefully discard the supernatant completely.

steps.title: 70% EtOH Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Ethanol, 99
steps.instruction: 70% EtOH Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Ethanol, 99.9% pure n/a 30 mL Sterilized purified water n/a 20 mL Total (optional) n/a 50 mL Note: This solution should be made up fresh on the day of the extraction. Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Filter p1000 tips (Elkay Laboratory Products, catalog number
steps.instruction: Filter p1000 tips (Elkay Laboratory Products, catalog number: AER-2REF-S

steps.title: Filter p200 tips (Elkay Laboratory Products, catalog number
steps.instruction: Filter p200 tips (Elkay Laboratory Products, catalog number: AER-REF-S

steps.title: In order to obtain the fungal...
steps.instruction: In order to obtain the fungal mycelial mass required for DNA extraction, place four mycelial plugs (6 mm in diameter) in 500 mL of potato dextrose broth (PDB) and grow for 7–14 days shaking at 140 rpm on an orbital shaker in a growth cabinet protected from light, at 22 °C.

steps.title: To reach the required gDNA concentration,...
steps.instruction: To reach the required gDNA concentration, up to 1 g of flash-frozen mycelium is required. When enough mycelium for the extraction plus backup material has grown, harvest ~3 g of mycelium using a 0.22 μm bottle top filter and vacuum pump.

steps.title: Rinse the mycelium with sterilized purified...
steps.instruction: Rinse the mycelium with sterilized purified water to remove any residue of PDB.

steps.title: Cut agar plugs from the harvested...
steps.instruction: Cut agar plugs from the harvested mycelium and place them in an autoclave bag. Flash freeze the harvested material in liquid nitrogen and store in a -80 °C freezer until ready to process. A

steps.title: Thaw the required amount of RNase...
steps.instruction: Thaw the required amount of RNase A and Proteinase K on ice, if kept frozen.

steps.title: Pre-cool freshly aliquoted propanol-2-OL in freezer at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Pre-cool freshly aliquoted propanol-2-OL in freezer at -20 °C.

steps.title: Pre-cool freshly aliquoted chloroform in freezer at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Pre-cool freshly aliquoted chloroform in freezer at -20 °C.

steps.title: Ensure mortars and pestles have been...
steps.instruction: Ensure mortars and pestles have been cleaned and sterilized in an autoclave at 121 °C for 15 min.

steps.title: Collect dry ice and keep in...
steps.instruction: Collect dry ice and keep in a polystyrene box in the fume hood.

steps.title: Preheat reagent 1 to 50 °C
steps.instruction: Preheat reagent 1 to 50 °C. B. Sample preparation

steps.title: Working over dry ice in a...
steps.instruction: Working over dry ice in a fume hood, grind 100 mg of flash-frozen mycelium with a sterilized mortar and pestle until a fine powder is formed. This powder should resemble talcum powder in consistency. Transfer this to a labeled 2 mL Eppendorf tube. Note: If working with low-yielding strains, run samples in quadruplicate and pool the extracts together when completed. This would require 400 mg of starting material to be ground. C. Cell lysis

steps.title: Add 600 μL of preheated Reagent...
steps.instruction: Add 600 μL of preheated Reagent 1 and 20 μL of RNase A to the sample in the 2 mL tube. Mix well with a vortex mixer or by inverting the tube 5–10 times, until the mycelium is homogenized with the buffers and enzymes. Ensure all the mycelium has been incorporated into the buffer. Incubate at 50 °C for 30 min, mixing at 450 rpm using the HC mixer. Note: For low-yielding strains, the lysis time can be extended overnight without harm to the DNA. Strains have been incubated for up to 12 h to improve yield.

steps.title: Once lysis incubation is completed, add...
steps.instruction: Once lysis incubation is completed, add 200 mL of Reagent 2 and mix by gentle inversion 5–10 times until a consistent solution is acquired.

steps.title: Add 10 μL of proteinase K...
steps.instruction: Add 10 μL of proteinase K (30 mg/mL = 600 U/mL) and incubate at 65 °C for 1 h with shaking at 450 rpm. D. DNA precipitation

steps.title: Place samples on ice for 20 min
steps.instruction: Place samples on ice for 20 min.

steps.title: While working in a fume hood,...
steps.instruction: While working in a fume hood, add 500 μL of pre-cooled chloroform and 100 μL of Cytiva Nucleon PhytoPure DNA extraction resin suspension. Mix by gentle rotation in a rotating mixer for 10 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge in a benchtop microfuge at...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge in a benchtop microfuge at 1,300× g for 10 min. Transfer the upper aqueous phase to a new, labeled 2 mL tube. Take care not to disturb the interface. Be conservative and leave some of the upper phase behind rather than carrying over chloroform or resin (expect ~500 μL).

steps.title: Add 1× volume of pre-cooled propanol-2-OL to each tube
steps.instruction: Add 1× volume of pre-cooled propanol-2-OL to each tube. Mix the tube by inversion 5–10 times and incubate at -20 °C for 15 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge in a benchtop microfuge at...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge in a benchtop microfuge at 4,000× g for 10 min. Discard the supernatant without disturbing the pellet. Leave the cap open on the tube and set it upright in a tube rack to air dry the sample for 2 min. E. DNA wash

steps.title: Wash the pellet with 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Wash the pellet with 1 mL of the prepared 70% EtOH. Invert the tube five times and centrifuge in a benchtop microfuge at 4,000× g for 20 min to collect the pellet.

steps.title: Pour off the ethanol without disturbing the pellet
steps.instruction: Pour off the ethanol without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Repeat the wash steps two more...
steps.instruction: Repeat the wash steps two more times for a total of three washes.

steps.title: Dry the sample by leaving the...
steps.instruction: Dry the sample by leaving the caps open for 5 min or until you no longer see droplets of ethanol present in the tube or around the pellet. Make sure to check the inside of the caps where ethanol can pool. Use a sterile medical wipe to carefully remove large droplets of ethanol on the caps or near the top of the tube. Do not put the medical wipe near the DNA pellet.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 100 μL of elution buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 100 μL of elution buffer. In our experience, it is better to let the pellet dissolve into the buffer by leaving it overnight in a refrigerator. Quantify the following day. Part II Macherey-Nagel NucleoBond HMW DNA kit modified protocol

steps.title: 70% EtOH Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Ethanol, 99
steps.instruction: 70% EtOH Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Ethanol, 99.9% pure n/a 30 mL Sterilized purified water n/a 20 mL Total (optional) n/a 50 mL Note: This solution should be made up fresh on the day of the extraction. Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Filter p1000 tips (Elkay Laboratory Products, catalog number
steps.instruction: Filter p1000 tips (Elkay Laboratory Products, catalog number: AER-2REF-S

steps.title: Filter p200 tips (Elkay Laboratory Products, catalog number
steps.instruction: Filter p200 tips (Elkay Laboratory Products, catalog number: AER-REF-S

steps.title: Prepare biological material as described in Method 1, step A
steps.instruction: Prepare biological material as described in Method 1, step A

steps.title: Heat the HC mixer to 55 °C
steps.instruction: Heat the HC mixer to 55 °C.

steps.title: Pre-chill sterilized mortar, pestle, measuring spoon,...
steps.instruction: Pre-chill sterilized mortar, pestle, measuring spoon, and a labeled 50 mL Falcon tube in a polystyrene box containing dry ice. Place this in the fume hood.

steps.title: Prepare 70% EtOH B
steps.instruction: Prepare 70% EtOH B. Sample preparation

steps.title: Working over dry ice in a...
steps.instruction: Working over dry ice in a fume hood, grind 1 g of flash-frozen mycelium using a sterilized mortar and pestle until a fine powder is formed. This powder should resemble talcum powder in consistenc y . Transfer this to a labeled 50 mL Falcon tube and keep on dry ice until ready to start lysis. C. Cell lysis

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.5 mL of preheated lysis buffer H1 and 100 μL of proteinase K to each tube. If more sample material needs to be processed (e.g., with low-yielding samples), increase volumes proportionally in this step and when adjusting the binding conditions.

steps.title: Vortex tubes for 5 s
steps.instruction: Vortex tubes for 5 s. Make sure that large amounts of the sample do not stick to the inner wall of the centrifuge tube.

steps.title: Incubate at 50 °C for 30...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 50 °C for 30 min, shaking at 450 rpm using the HC mixer. Note: For low-yielding strains, the lysis time can be extended overnight without harm to the DNA. Strains have been incubated for up to 12 h to improve yield. D. RNA digestion

steps.title: Once lysis incubation is completed, add...
steps.instruction: Once lysis incubation is completed, add 50 μL of RNase A to each tube and mix by inversion until a consistent solution is acquired.

steps.title: Incubate for 5 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 min at room temperature. E. Column steps

steps.title: For each sample, combine a Macherey-Nagel...
steps.instruction: For each sample, combine a Macherey-Nagel NucleoBond HMW column (including filter) with a plastic washer and arrange the combination on a labeled 50 mL Falcon tube. Set these in a stand. While setting this up, ensure that the plastic washer does not seal the waste vessel airtight as stated in the kit’s protocol.

steps.title: Equilibrate the filters and columns by...
steps.instruction: Equilibrate the filters and columns by adding 12 mL of buffer H2 to the upper rim of the column filters and make sure the complete filter and the silica matrix are pre-wet.

steps.title: Let gravity perform these steps while using these columns
steps.instruction: Let gravity perform these steps while using these columns. Do not use a vacuum.

steps.title: Add 10 mL of buffer H2...
steps.instruction: Add 10 mL of buffer H2 to each sample and mix by inverting the tube gently 5 times. (If you added more starting material at the start of this protocol, this is where you will need to increase volume proportionally.)

steps.title: Load samples, trying to avoid any...
steps.instruction: Load samples, trying to avoid any debris into the center of the column filters, and let the lysate pass the silica matrix by gravity flow.

steps.title: Add 6 mL of buffer H3...
steps.instruction: Add 6 mL of buffer H3 to the rim of the column filters and let the buffer pass the filter and the silica.

steps.title: Discard the flowthrough and the column...
steps.instruction: Discard the flowthrough and the column filter once the buffer has passed the silica.

steps.title: Wash the column without filter with...
steps.instruction: Wash the column without filter with 12 mL of buffer H

steps.title: Place the HMW column on top...
steps.instruction: Place the HMW column on top of a clean and labeled 50 mL Falcon tube.

steps.title: Elute DNA with 5 mL of buffer H
steps.instruction: Elute DNA with 5 mL of buffer H

steps.title: Using a wide-bore 1,000 μL pipette...
steps.instruction: Using a wide-bore 1,000 μL pipette tip, divide the DNA solution into 5 × 2 mL tubes (1,000 μL/tube). Use caution when pipetting to keep from shearing your long strands of DNA.

steps.title: Add 1,000 μL of chilled propanol-2-OL to each tube
steps.instruction: Add 1,000 μL of chilled propanol-2-OL to each tube.

steps.title: Mix by carefully and slowly inverting the tube 10 times
steps.instruction: Mix by carefully and slowly inverting the tube 10 times.

steps.title: Using a sterilized glass rod, spool...
steps.instruction: Using a sterilized glass rod, spool out visible DNA strands into a labeled tube with 50 μL of Low TE or HE elution buffer. There is no need to wash spooled DNA strands. Set this into the fridge when finished. (This is the final tube into which you will pool the washed DNA once completing steps K5, K6, L1–L7, and M1–M

steps.title: Centrifuge what remains in the tubes...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge what remains in the tubes after twisting out noticeable DNA at 5,000× g for 10 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Carefully discard the supernatant
steps.instruction: Carefully discard the supernatant. Pellets will be difficult to see. G. Wash DNA pellet

steps.title: Add 500 μL of 70% EtOH...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μL of 70% EtOH solution to each DNA pellet.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 5,000× g for 5 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 5,000× g for 5 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Add 25 μL of 70% EtOH...
steps.instruction: Add 25 μL of 70% EtOH to each DNA pellet for a second wash.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 7,000× g for 5 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 7,000× g for 5 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Place tubes with the lids open...
steps.instruction: Place tubes with the lids open upright in a tray to dry the pellet by incubating at room temperature until the ethanol has evaporated (~5 min). Check the inside of the lids of the tubes for excess droplets. Using sterilized medical tissue, dab any ethanol droplets. Do not overdry the pellet, as this can lead to degradation of the DNA and difficulties in resuspension. H. Resuspend DNA

steps.title: Add ≤20 μL of low TE...
steps.instruction: Add ≤20 μL of low TE or HE elution buffer to the DNA pellets in each tube and carefully resuspend the DNA by gently swirling the tube. Make sure the pellet is immersed in the buffer. Note: Remember you have 50 μL of the spooled DNA from step K4 already in the fridge. This will give you a final volume of 150 μL.

steps.title: Leave DNA overnight in the refrigerator...
steps.instruction: Leave DNA overnight in the refrigerator (4 °C) for complete resuspension. In the morning, combine the DNA into your final tube (to which you have already added the DNA strands previously).

steps.title: Use wide-bore pipette tips for all...
steps.instruction: Use wide-bore pipette tips for all further downstream applications when handling DNA. I. DNA assessment

steps.title: Use a NanoDrop to assess the...
steps.instruction: Use a NanoDrop to assess the purity of the DNA after letting the pellets rest in elution buffer overnight.

steps.title: Check the amount of DNA using...
steps.instruction: Check the amount of DNA using a Qubit HS (high sensitivity) or BR (broad range) assay.

steps.title: Check the length and integrity of...
steps.instruction: Check the length and integrity of the strands using a Femto analyzer.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol has been used and validated in the following research article: • Hill et al. [1]. Evolutionary genomics reveals variation in structure and genetic content implicated in virulence and lifestyle in the genus Gaeumannomyces. DNA quantity and quality were assessed using three techniques for nucleic acid quantification: UV spectrophotometry using a NanoDrop spectrophotometer, dsDNA specific fluorimetry using a Qubit fluorometer, and DNA integrity and length of strands using a Femto Pulse parallel capillary electrophoresis system. DNA purity was assessed by reading A 260/230nm and A 260/280nm on the NanoDrop instrument ( Table 1 ). When determining the purity of each extraction, it is important to acknowledge the difference between the Qubit DNA concentration readings and the Nanodrop. Differences greater than 5% indicate impurity in the DNA extracts. On average, the Cytiva Nucleon PhytoPure kit produces greater differences than the Macherey-Nagel NucleoBond HMW DNA kit ( Table 1 ). After assessing whether DNA concentration and purity pass minimum requirements, the relative success of each extraction for sequencing is then determined by assessing strand length, which impacts downstream library preparation ( Table 1 ). Samples with a greater proportion of shorter strands (at a cutoff of 40 kbp) are constructed using the 10–15 kb library preparation, and those with a greater proportion of larger strands are constructed using the 15–20 kb library preparation kit. Strand length will impact genome assembly contiguity. However, given the strand lengths attained here and the downstream biological questions, low input DNA extraction and shorter fragment length libraries did not impact our ability to address questions on genome evolution [6] (see Table S2). Table 1. DNA quantity and quality mean (SD) values obtained from the Cytiva Nucleon PhytoPure kit (n = 11 each with quadruple replicates per the sample, pooled together) and the Macherey-Nag
